subject: Dia Bom
body:
Querido,
Meu nome é Nora Alex. Gostaria de saber sobre seus serviços para ser meu
sócio/investidor estrangeiro e me apoiar a transferir e administrar meus
fundos em uma empresa lucrativa e como comprar ações ou imóveis pela
empresa, tenho uma quantia razoável que herdei de meu pai que eu quero que
você me ajude a investir em seu país.
Observação: abaixo está o principal motivo pelo qual estou entrando em
contato com você.
(1) Para fornecer uma nova conta bancária vazia na qual esse dinheiro seria
transferido. se você não quiser que usemos sua conta pessoal.
(2) Para servir como guardião deste fundo, já que o banco insistiu que o
acordo com meu pai era que eu fornecesse um sócio estrangeiro antes de
liberar o fundo.
(3) Para providenciar minha vinda ao seu país para continuar meus estudos e
obter uma autorização de residência em seu país.
responda-me assim que ler esta mensagem para que eu saiba como proceder.
Obrigado seu mais gentil,
Senhorita Nora Alex.
model_reason: The message is a classic example of a phishing scam, where the sender is trying to establish a relationship and gain trust in order to ask for financial assistance or investment opportunities.

body: Customer has cash flow issues. Can we offer payment plan?
model_reason: Legitimate email with a clear subject and body, no suspicious links or attachments.

subject: Urgent Help Needed: Mercury Language Integration Issues
body:
Hi Aetheros Support,

We are facing critical issues with integrating Mercury language into our existing application. The documentation seems outdated, and we are experiencing frequent errors that we are unable to debug.

We are on a tight deadline and this is severely impacting our development. We need immediate assistance to resolve these issues.

Please let us know what information you need from our side to help us faster.

Thanks,
John Doe
model_reason: Lack of urgency and specificity in the request, along with a generic greeting and signature.

subject: Mercury Language Documentation Question
body:
Hello Aetheros Support,

I'm working with the Mercury language for our API development, and I'm having trouble finding clear documentation on how to implement custom error handling. Specifically, I want to define unique error codes and messages to return from my API.

Could you point me to the relevant documentation or provide an example?

Thanks,
John Smith
model_reason: Legitimate email with clear subject and context

subject: Office Support_Desk: Restore Failed Deliveries
body: This phish typically originates from a non-Cornell account. The sender may appear as "Expedited Retrieval <[user]@skytechcontrol.io>". The link within directs to a fake login page. Do not follow the link nor contact the sender. If you entered your credentials into the linked form, change your NetID password immediately at https://netid.cornell.edu .
model_reason: The message explicitly warns about a phishing attempt and provides a legitimate link to change the password.

subject: Will renters rise up in Toronto’s election?
body: First Up Plus: Who gets the final say on a listing price? ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ LIVING HERE By Amber Shortt Good morning. Here’s the latest on cottage country’s new normal, how renters could shape the Toronto election and who should get final say on a listing price. GOOD WEEK FOR... Homebuilders. A jump in sales shows buyers are trickling back to the GTA’s newly built home market. But, as BILD’s CEO says, now is not the time to call the market “anywhere near being balanced” — we still need more housing. Homebuyers. Bidding wars are back — but if you have the means and a strong stomach, the Star’s David Olive says it’s a good time to get into the market. Here's why this time is different. Dockside deskers. Elon Musk may still have a few fans, as hybrid workers in Ontario’s cottage country hail his satellite internet service as a “game changer.” It’s one factor in cottage country’s new normal. Where should you eat next? If you’re wondering where to eat as the city heats up, you need Food Crawl, the Star’s free food newsletter. Whether it’s a strip mall standout or a downtown gem, Food Crawl will make you an insider on the best eats in Toronto and beyond. Sign up for free here. BAD WEEK FOR... Caledon residents, who are caught in the middle of the Peel Region divorce. It’s meant to help speed up housing, but sorting out the details will likely be a headache. Indebted Canadians. Canadians have racked up more household debt than any other G7 country, with about three-quarters stemming from mortgages — and it will likely get worse. Young investors. Not surprisingly, a new Statistics Canada report shows the real estate investment market is dominated by boomers, with the number of homes investor-owned at 15 per cent. Here’s who else is investing in Canadian real estate. TRENDING During the last municipal election, a candidate knocked on my door and told my partner not to sell our house, listing all the reasons property values in our former city of York neighbourhood would be rising. And when my partner told him we were renting? “Oh,” he said. “That’s too bad.” I’d say I can’t blame him for being surprised. To most people, the idea of a renter conjures up an image of an undergrad student in a basement eating Kraft Dinner — not a family of four in a detached house, well, also eating Kraft Dinner (parents of three-year-olds, you get it). But not only is the number of renting households increasing, it’s also becoming more diverse — meaning renters could be a force in next month’s mayoral election. “It’s everyone — young professionals, families, seniors — and they’re all struggling because Toronto is not an affordable city to live in,” tenant advocate Douglas Kwan told the Star’s Victoria Gibson and May Warren this week. Tenants’ frustration is mounting over rising rents, threats of eviction and, for some, the lost dream of home ownership as prices rise again. Mayoral hopefuls this time around are waking up to the notion that the 48 per cent of Toronto households who pay rent instead of a mortgage could decide their fate. But as policies to increase housing affordability roll off candidates’ tongues, the big question may not be whether renters vote, but will they vote together? As University of Toronto professor Matti Siemiatycki told the Star, it’s not a foregone conclusion that renters will vote as a block, saying, “Whether it’s urban and suburban, or motorist and cyclist and pedestrian, there are all sorts of other dynamics that people see as important to their identity and interests.” Still, as the housing crisis deepens, any candidate who ignores renters’ concerns may just find tenants have no interest in giving them a four-year lease on the mayor’s seat. And that’s too bad. LOCATION, LOCATION,LOCATION NEWS FROM AROUND THE GTA AND BEYOND “Drive until you qualify” is pushing home hunters farther from the city and to places like Bradford, Ont., where this home doubled in price in a decade. The Residences of the Well at Front St. and Spadina Ave. can only be rented, not bought by investors. One renter, life coach Katia Stern, shares why it works for her. This Richmond Street East building now boasts Canada’s first residential air-to-water heat pump — technology that could make all condos carbon-free. Check it out. Remember that Kensington laneway home with a rooftop shower? It’s still for sale for a slightly lower asking price of just under $3 million. Soak it in. [Realtor.ca] Whitby’s heritage committee decided a home built in the late 1800s, with no discernible historical value, can be torn down for a new housing complex. [DurhamRegion.com] In Vienna’s Gemeindebauten (social housing), the proportion of tenants’ incomes that goes to rent is in the single digits. What can Toronto learn? [The New York Times] SHOULD I OVERRULE MY AGENT ON LIST PRICE? ADVICE FROM THE EXPERTS The Star talked to Property.ca broker Julian Kashani about how agents decide on a list price, and what happens when an agent and seller disagree. Here’s what he said: When suggesting a price to a client, I usually do a comparative market analysis to get a sense of what similar properties have recently sold for. I even take my client to other currently listed properties in their area and show them the pluses and minuses of a property compared to their own. If we do disagree on price, I advise we price it a little below what we think the property is worth but do an offer date — only accepting offers on one day — and see what the market will offer. This sales strategy is often done in hot sellers’ markets and when it is hard to price a property because it is unique. If the client wants to list at a higher price, thinking we can lower it later if needed, I explain they risk staying in the market too long, which could lead buyers to wonder if there’s something wrong with the property. An agent’s job is to give their client the best possible information, but at the end of the day it’s their property and the price is their choice. Have a burning real estate question of your own? Email us at livinghere@thestar.ca. Thanks for reading. Before you go, here’s a look inside the moated community of Toronto Islands. Living Here will be back in your inbox next week. Get unlimited access to the Star Limited time sale: Only $1/week for 52 weeks Get This Offer If you're not enjoying these emails, please tell us how we can make them better by emailing newsletterfeedback@thestar.ca. Or, if you'd prefer, you can unsubscribe from this newsletter by clicking the first link below. Link: https://forms.gle/mndSfjB3kXSWmVJU8 Invalidating Tax Assessments UNCOVER THE STRATEGIES, TACTICS AND REMEDIES IN INVALIDATING TAX ASSESSMENT June 18, 2025 2:00pm - 5:00pm *LIVE VIA ZOOM* Introduction: Are you or is your business being assessed (or likely to be assessed) by the B.I.R.? Have you received any notice (Letter of Authority, Tax Verification Notice, Subpoena Duces Tecum, and the like)? Are you prepared? You need to be aware of what exactly you need to do or you will stand to spend an arm and a leg. Uncover the strategies, tactics, and remedies in handling tax assessments, disputes, and claims for refunds. Course Objectives: This course is designed specifically for individuals and businesses currently facing tax assessments by the Bureau of Internal Revenue (B.I.R.) or those who anticipate an impending assessment. Participants will gain practical insights into strategies for addressing tax assessments, contesting erroneous claims, and pursuing tax refunds. By the end of the course, participants will be empowered to effectively manage and navigate their tax challenges, minimizing financial and legal risks. Major Takeaways: Develop strategies to respond to B.I.R. assessments and potential disputes. Recognize critical assessment stages and appropriate reactions to notices received. Identify common audit findings and learn how to effectively challenge them. Understand the tax refund process and the necessary steps for successful claims. Course Content Assessing Your Tax Assessment Situation a. Legal basis of authority to assess Taxpayer b. Prescriptive period of Tax Assessments * 3 years vs 10 years prescriptive period * Extension of period to assess through the issuance of waiver c. Types of BIR audit/investigation notices * Letter of authority, Letter Notice, Tax Verification Notice and Mission Order * Service of assessment notices d. Immediate Steps to Take Upon Receiving a Notice * Key actions to undertake to protect your interests. e. Submission of documents pursuant to BIR notice * Issuance of subpoena duces tecum * Application of the best evidence rule Understanding the Stages of Tax Assessment a. Notice of discrepancy (NOD) b. Preliminary Assessment Notice (PAN) Best practices for responding to a PAN to avoid a final assessment. c. Final Assessment Notice (FAN) What to do when you receive a FAN and how to build your case. d. Final Decision Disputed Assessment (FDA) Strategies for navigating the final resolution process and implications for your business. Challenging Common Audit Findings a. Questioning the validity of tax assessment at different stages of tax assessment Overview of frequent discrepancies found during audits and how they affect your assessment. b. Addressing factual and legal issues in tax assessment Crafting compelling arguments and gathering supporting documentation to challenge the assessment. Claiming Tax Refunds a. Understanding Refund Claims and Their Requirements Types of claims: Erroneously paid taxes versus overpaid input VAT. Identifying the prescriptive periods for claims and key documentation requirements. b. Rule on exhaustion of administrative remedies Knowing the procedural steps necessary to pursue your rights effectively. Featured Speaker: MR. SENEN QUIZON Tax Partner - Deloitte Philippines Senen has over 17 years of professional experience in tax services. His experience covers tax ruling assistance, tax investigation assistance, tax training, and tax consultancy for companies engaged in the Manufacturing, Life Sciences & Health Care, and Technology, Media & Telecommunications industries. Prior to joining Deloitte, he was the Chief of the Direct Taxes Branch of a government agency, where he conducted numerous studies in the area of direct tax and tax administration. During his stint in the government, he participated in the legislative process by drafting proposed tax legislations and serving as a resource person in public hearings on various tax measures. Senen is an Accredited Tax Practitioner of the Bureau of Internal Revenue and is a regular speaker at the Philippine Institute of Certified Public Accountants (PICPA). He worked for 10 years in another local accounting firm as part of its Tax Knowledge Center, which handles training and publications. He earned his degree in Bachelor of Science in Applied Economics from the Polytechnic University of the Philippines and received his Master’s degree in Business Administration from Colegio de San Juan de Letran.
